quality: The dance of existence — creation and destruction as the same act
visual_signature: Standing on demon Apasmara — dancing within a ring of fire
story_role: The one who creates by destroying — transformation through total loss
character_archetype: The hero at the moment of their greatest sacrifice
notes: For the final act of a story — the moment everything changes

quality: Time that devours all — even the gods — yet remains beyond time
visual_signature: Dark blue/black — standing in cremation grounds
story_role: The force that makes all things temporary — and therefore precious
character_archetype: The villain who understands impermanence better than the hero
notes: Villains who believe they are accelerating inevitable end

quality: Righteous terrifying anger — the consequence of real transgression
visual_signature: Blood-red — wild hair — hunting dogs as companions
story_role: The punishment that arrives when all warnings have been ignored
character_archetype: The hero in their most terrifying righteous anger
notes: For moment when hero stops holding back

quality: Unity of Shiva and Shakti — no power without its complement
visual_signature: Literally half male half female — split body
story_role: The truth that every hero contains both their power and its shadow
character_archetype: Heroes who discover their strength by accepting their vulnerability
notes: For character growth arcs — the hero becoming whole

quality: Guru who teaches through silence — knowledge transmitted without words
visual_signature: Young-faced but ancient — seated under banyan tree — four arms
story_role: The guru who teaches more by what they don't say
character_archetype: The mentor figure who makes the hero find their own answer
notes: Model for the guru characters in Hulmane-The-Saga

quality: Sovereign of all living things — the power that cannot be domesticated
visual_signature: Proto-Shiva seal — surrounded by animals in meditation
story_role: The one the wild things obey — the power before civilization
character_archetype: Heroes with deep animal bonds — especially forest states
notes: Jharkhand and Chhattisgarh heroes most connected

quality: The one strong enough to receive the full force of the Ganga — breaking its fall
symbol_or_weapon: Matted hair that holds the river
visual_signature: Ganga flowing from the matted hair
story_role: The one who absorbs what would destroy others — protection through reception
character_archetype: Heroes who bear impossible burdens for their people

quality: Destroying the three demon cities — the illusion that has become too powerful
symbol_or_weapon: Single arrow from a cosmic bow
visual_signature: Arrow of dharmic fire — mountains as bow — Shiva seated on a lotus
story_role: The moment the false world is burned away and reality is revealed
character_archetype: Tripura hero (Tripurari) is directly named. Also the climax archetype.
notes: The villain who built an illusion-fortress will always face this moment

quality: The ascetic power of mountains — silence that speaks louder than action
symbol_or_weapon: Trident — surrounded by snow and ice
visual_signature: Seated in deep snow at the peak — eternally unmoved
story_role: The unchanging centre while everything around spins
character_archetype: Uttarakhand and Himachal Pradesh heroes most connected
notes: The guru in mountain kingdoms often reflects this form

quality: The power over death itself — the sacred syllables that hold death at bay
visual_signature: Moon on the forehead — third eye — garland of snakes
story_role: The one who faces death not by avoiding it but by understanding it
character_archetype: Heroes who survive the unsurvivable — who come back changed

quality: Drinking the poison that would destroy the world — absorbing darkness to protect others
symbol_or_weapon: The blue throat — visible sign of the sacrifice
visual_signature: Blue/purple throat — otherwise white — divine and human simultaneously
story_role: The leader who takes the poison so others don't have to
character_archetype: Heroes who take on the consequences of others' choices
notes: Karnataka and Kerala heroes reflect this quality

quality: The storm that destroys and heals — you cannot have the rain without the lightning
visual_signature: Wild hair flying — storm clouds — animals fleeing and following
story_role: The force that is neither good nor evil — simply power seeking its own level
character_archetype: Villains who are elemental forces rather than personal antagonists
notes: For the Void Maw itself — Rudra as cosmic storm
